# OSS Move Mode â€” Hold & Drag (incl. Masked-Layer Movement): Behavior + JS Fidelity Gaps

> Authoritative engineering spec. Every behavioral claim is cited `file:line`. OSS = OpenStrandStudio (`src/move_mode.py`, `layer_state_manager.py`, `masked_strand.py`, `strand.py`, `strand_drawing_canvas.py`, `undo_redo_manager.py`). JS = OpenStrandJS (`src/modes/MoveMode.ts`, `src/interaction/*`, `src/store/*`, `web/strand-renderer.js`).

---

## 1. Overview â€” the mental model

Move mode is a single press â†’ drag â†’ release gesture over a static document. The two phases have distinct jobs:

- **Hold (press) = select/grab.** On mouse-down OSS decides *exactly one* thing the user is touching â€” a control point, or an endpoint of one strand â€” using a fixed-priority, multi-pass hit-test (`move_mode.py:1128`, `1223-1241`, `1814-1900`). The grab seeds `affected_strand`, `moving_side`, a cursor-locking `mouse_offset`, and (for endpoint grabs) the *whole connected chain that will move together* (`gather_moving_strands`, `move_mode.py:3579-3644`). Control-point grabs move only the one strand (`start_movement`, `move_mode.py:2543-2547`).
- **Drag (move) = reposition.** Each mouse-move computes an **absolute** target = `snap(cursor + grab_offset)` (not a delta), assigns it to the grabbed point, and assigns the *same absolute position* to every peer endpoint whose own directed connection slot points back at the grabbed `(affected_strand, moving_side)` (`update_strand_position`, `move_mode.py:2702`, `2950-2965`). Angle is free; the only quantization is grid snapping.

The structural invariants a faithful port must preserve:

1. **Directed single-slot connection graph** decides what-moves-together (not a symmetric weld component). See Â§3.
2. **MaskedStrands are never grabbed by clicking the canvas** â€” they move only as a *side effect* of a constituent strand moving, and their geometry is **re-derived** every frame, not stored. See Â§5.
3. **One drag = at most one undo step**, committed externally on release (`undo_redo_manager.py:3040-3060`). See Â§6.

JS implements the same skeleton: `MoveMode.onPointerDown` â†’ `hitTest` â†’ `beginGesture`/`setDragging`/`beginWeldGesture` (`MoveMode.ts:29-54`); `onPointerMove` â†’ `snapPoint` â†’ `mutateDoc(moveHandle)` (`MoveMode.ts:56-74`, `actions.ts:23-93`); `onPointerUp` â†’ `endWeldGesture`/`commit` (`MoveMode.ts:76-86`). The fidelity gaps (Â§7) are concentrated in **grab priority/geometry**, **selection semantics on body/blank clicks**, **mask deletion-rectangle tracking**, and **snap zoom/Ctrl gating**.

---

## 2. Press / Grab

### 2.1 Press sequence (OSS `mousePressEvent`, `move_mode.py:1128`)

```
pos = event.pos()                                    # canvas/world coords (1131)
selection_at_press_start = selected_strand or selected_attached_strand   # (1141)
start hold_timer(16ms) + redraw_timer; force first redraw                # (1144-1154)
if draw_only_affected_strand: prepare_optimized_drawing()                # (1157-1158)
if not in_move_mode:
    originally_selected_strand = ...; LSM.start_op(); save_state();
    build_connection_cache(); in_move_mode = True                        # (1163-1202)

# PASS 1 â€” CONTROL POINTS (global, forward, wins over everything)
for strand in canvas.strands:                                            # (1223)
    if deleted or is_hidden: continue
    if not _is_strand_allowed_by_selection(strand): continue
    if try_move_control_points(strand, pos, event):                      # (1226)
        selected_strand = selected_attached_strand = None; update(); return   # (1234-1241)

snapshot is_selected of all strands                                      # (1247-1251)
handle_strand_movement(pos, event)                                       # (1254) â€” endpoint hit-test

if is_moving:                                                            # grab happened
    last_update_pos=pos; accumulated_delta=0; last_snapped_pos=snap(pos) # (1373-1376)
    ...set temp_selected_strand, mark is_selected, set selected_attached_strand  # (1406-1418)
else:                                                                    # blank-space click
    for strand: strand.is_selected = (strand == selection_at_press_start)   # (1431-1432)
    restore canvas selection refs from selection_at_press_start             # (1434-1440)
update()                                                                 # (1446)
```

### 2.2 Three hit-test passes â€” winner is whoever fires first

| Pass | Function | Order | Areas | Wins over |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Control points (global) | `try_move_control_points` loop, `move_mode.py:1223-1241`; redundant re-pass `1814-1822` | **FORWARD** (bottom layer first) | 50Ã—50 `QRectF` (`get_control_point_rectangle`, `2136-2153`) | **Everything** â€” returns before any endpoint test |
| 2. Connection-aware endpoints | `handle_strand_movement`, `1829-1887` | **FORWARD** | 120Ã—120 `QPainterPath` square (`get_start_area`/`get_end_area`, `2155-2206`), gated by `can_move_side` | Falls back to pass 3 only if no endpoint area hit |
| 3. Isolated/topmost endpoints | `reversed(enumerate(strands))`, `1889-1900` â†’ `try_move_strand` (`2099-2134`) | **REVERSE** (topmost first) | same 120Ã—120 squares; `start_area` before `end_area` | â€” |

**Pass 2 detail (the "prefer parent at a joint" rule).** For each strand whose `get_start_area`/`get_end_area` contains `pos` (and passes `can_move_side`), OSS appends `(strand, side, area)` PLUS every `get_connected_strands(layer_name, side)` peer (LSM) to `connection_point_strands`. After the loop it **prefers the first entry that is NOT an `AttachedStrand`** (the main/parent strand) â€” `main_strand_entry` (`1873-1884`); else `connection_point_strands[0]`. So at a parent+child weld the **parent** is grabbed even if the click is geometrically nearer the child or the child is topmost.

### 2.3 What `start_movement` sets (`move_mode.py:2362`, key lines `2400-2451`)

```
moving_side = side
strand_pos = { 0: start, 1: end,
               'control_point1': cp1, 'control_point2': cp2,
               'control_point_center': cpc, 'bias_control': click }[side]   # (2404-2418)
initial_mouse_pos = click;  mouse_offset = strand_pos - click               # (2421-2427) â€” no cursor jump
moving_point = strand_pos;  affected_strand = strand;  is_moving = True      # (2440-2444)
is_moving_control_point = side in {control_point1/2/center, bias_*}          # (2449)
is_moving_strand_point  = side in {0,1}                                      # (2451)
last_snapped_pos = target_pos = strand_pos                                   # (2527-2528)
if side == 'control_point1': auto_adjust_control_points(strand)             # (2517-2518)
truly_moving_strands = gather_moving_strands(strand, side) if side in {0,1}  # (2543-2547)
                       else [strand]
```

### 2.4 Control-point side encodings (`try_move_control_points`, `move_mode.py:1945-2097`)

Order: **bias** (if enabled â†’ `'bias_triangle'/'bias_circle'/'bias_control'`, `1984-2012`) â†’ **cp1** (`'control_point1'`, sets `triangle_has_moved=True` as a press side-effect, `2045-2057`) â†’ **cp2** (only if `control_point2_shown`, `2058-2071`) â†’ **cp3 center** (only if `enable_third_control_point` AND `triangle_has_moved`, `2080-2096`). Masked/hidden/locked early-return `False` (`1959-1972`).

### 2.5 Click areas / scaling

- Endpoints: **120Ã—120 square** (`outer_size=120`, half=60), `QPainterPath.contains` (`2155-2206`). Effective area 14400 pxÂ², corners reach ~84.85 px diagonal.
- Control points: **50Ã—50 square** (`size=50`), `QRectF.contains` (`2136-2153`). cp3 returns an *empty* `QRectF` when the third-CP feature is off (never matches).
- Constants are **fixed in canvas coords**; the global `zoom_factor` transform scales their apparent screen size. They do **not** scale with strand width or grid size (`2138`).

### 2.6 Lock / selection gating

- `set_locked_layers` stores `locked_layers` (a set of indices) + `lock_mode_active` (`146-156`).
- `can_move_side` (`2331-2360`): index-locked strand â†’ `False`; for sides 0/1 a click coinciding with a **locked** strand's start/end is blocked (a shared joint with a locked neighbor is frozen); control-point sides always pass.
- `_is_strand_allowed_by_selection` (`1797-1801`): with `move_selected_only`/`show_cp_selected_only` active, grabs are restricted to the selected family (and if nothing is selected, **nothing** is grabbable).

### 2.7 Masks at press

MaskedStrand is **completely excluded** from press grabbing: control-point pass (`1960-1961`), both endpoint passes (`1816/1830/1891`), `try_move_strand` (`2113-2114`), and hover (`2245/2306`). Therefore `affected_strand`/`moving_side` can **never** be set from a mask by clicking. The MaskedStrand branches in `mousePressEvent` (`1379-1405`) and `start_movement` (`2565-2570`) are **dead code for press-driven grabs**. A mask is selected/moved only via the layer panel.

### 2.8 JS press (`MoveMode.onPointerDown`, `hitTest`)

```
hit = hitTest(p.world, doc, settings)                       # MoveMode.ts:31
if hit.kind === 'handle':
    drag = {layer, handle, offset = handlePos(s,handle) - p.world}   # :32-35
    setSelection; beginGesture(); setDragging(true); beginWeldGesture();
    setDragMoving([...movingStrandSet(doc, layer, handle)])          # :36-45
elif hit.kind === 'body': setSelection({layerName, handle:null})    # :47-49  (selects body)
else: setSelection({layerName:null, handle:null})                   # :50-52  (deselects)
```

`hitTest` (`hitTest.ts:51-73`) runs **two reverse-z (topmost-first) passes**: Pass 1 handles (`strandHandles` priority `cp_center` â†’ `cp1` â†’ `cp2` â†’ `start` â†’ `end`, circular radii `ENDPOINT_R=60`/`CP_R=25`); Pass 2 bodies (`distToPolyline <= width/2+stroke+2`). This per-strand interleaving and topmost-first ordering, the circular grab areas, the body-select, and the deselect-on-blank are the source of the Â§7 grab gaps.

---

## 3. What-Moves-Together â€” the directed single-slot connection graph

### 3.1 The table

For every **non-masked** strand `S`, `connections[S] = [start_formatted, end_formatted]`, each entry `'null'` or `'name(p)'` = "S's start(slot 0)/end(slot 1) connects to strand `name`, whose connecting endpoint is `p` (0=name.start, 1=name.end)". Built by `LayerStateManager.get_layer_connections` (`layer_state_manager.py:239-342`). MaskedStrands are skipped as keys (`245-246`) and as neighbors at every source.

### 3.2 Precedence (slot-filling order â€” the heart of the model)

| Priority | Source | Slot it claims | Guard | OSS ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (overridable) | **PARENT** â€” S's own start slot (slot 0) | slot 0 = `parent(attachment_side)` | always (parent skipped if masked) | `262-284` |
| 2 (first-claim-wins) | **CHILDREN** of S â€” each claims S's slot it attaches to | child w/ `attachment_side==0`â†’slot 0; `==1`â†’slot 1; recorded endpoint = 0 (child's start) | **`if slot is None`** â€” a filled slot is NOT overwritten | `288-303` |
| 3 (highest effective) | **KNOTS** (`knot_connections`) | slot 0 or 1 = neighbor end (0 if 'start' else 1) | **NO guard â€” unconditional overwrite** | `318-332` |

Each slot is **single-valued**: a strand touching three others at one point still records **one** neighbor per slot. This is the directed single-slot first-claim-wins behavior (vs symmetric union-find). The table is frozen during a drag (`getConnections` returns `cached_connections`, `layer_state_manager.py:538-548`).

### 3.3 Lookup, BFS gather, and the move-test (pseudocode)

```python
def get_connected(name, point):                  # at most ONE neighbor (move_mode.py:3534-3577)
    if point not in {0,1}: return []
    e = connections[name][point]
    if e == 'null': return []
    nname, nend = parse(e)                        # 'name(p)'
    return [(object(nname), nend)]                # carries neighbor's recorded endpoint

def gather_moving(initial, moving_point):         # BFS over (strand, side) (move_mode.py:3579-3644)
    if moving_point not in {0,1}: return [initial]    # control point â†’ only the one strand
    visited = set(); out = []; Q = [(initial, moving_point)]
    while Q:
        cur, pt = Q.pop(0)
        if (cur.name, pt) in visited: continue        # visited keyed by (name, point)
        visited.add((cur.name, pt)); out.append(cur)
        for nbr, nend in get_connected(cur.name, pt):
            if (nbr.name, nend) not in visited:
                Q.append((nbr, nend))                 # leaves from neighbor's RECORDED end
    return dedupe_by_identity(out)                    # candidate set = truly_moving_strands

# THE MOVE-TEST, re-evaluated every mouse-move (move_mode.py:2950-2965)
for cand in truly_moving_strands:
    if cand is affected_strand: continue
    move_start = (affected_strand, moving_side) in get_connected(cand.name, 0)
    move_end   = (affected_strand, moving_side) in get_connected(cand.name, 1)
    if move_start: cand.start = new_pos               # its slot0 points BACK â†’ start follows
    if move_end:   cand.end   = new_pos               # its slot1 points BACK â†’ end follows
    cand.update_shape(); cand.update_side_line()
```

Being in the gathered list is **necessary but not sufficient**: a candidate's endpoint moves only if **its own slot points back at exactly `(affected_strand, moving_side)`**. This back-pointing test (geometry-free) is what guarantees only the genuinely-wired endpoint snaps.

### 3.4 JS implementation (`src/interaction/connections.ts`)

`buildConnTable` (`57-106`) mirrors the precedence exactly: PARENT via `set()` (`77-83`), CHILDREN via `setIfEmpty` first-claim-wins (`87-92`), KNOTS via `set()` overwrite (`95-102`); MaskedStrands skipped at every step (`65,74,79,89,98`). `gatherMoving` (`145-159`) is the BFS; `connectedMovers` (`164-179`) is the move-test (peer endpoint follows only if its slot points back at `(primary, side)`). `gestureConnTable` (`132-140`) freezes the table for the gesture. `movingStrandSet` (`193-208`) builds a deliberate **superset** of the moved endpoints (adds masks whose components move) for the renderer bake-around fast-path. The header (`connections.ts:1-30`) documents this as a faithful port of `gather_moving_strands` + the move-test.

> **Confirmed grab-side consequence (not a connections bug):** because the table is **asymmetric**, the BFS *root* `(primary, side)` determines the moved set. JS's grab picks a different root than OSS at parent+child welds (topmost child vs parent) and at overlapping handles (topmost vs forward) â€” see Â§7 grab gaps â€” so the *same* faithful table can yield a *different* moved set.

---

## 4. Drag Math

### 4.1 `new_pos` is ABSOLUTE, not a delta

```
adjusted = (mouse_offset != 0) ? cursor + mouse_offset : cursor     # move_mode.py:4022-4028
new_pos  = snap(adjusted)                                           # see 4.2
```

`mouse_offset = strand_point - click` captured once at grab (`2426-2427`) keeps the held point under the cursor's original grab spot. Every connected peer is assigned the **identical** `new_pos` object â€” exact coincidence, not parallel translation (`2962-2965`).

### 4.2 Snapping / angle (OSS `mouseMoveEvent`, `move_mode.py:4036-4079`)

Snapping is the **only** quantization â€” **there is NO 45Â° lock in move mode** (the 45Â° lock lives only in strand creation). The 5-way zoom/Ctrl-gated decision:

| Condition | Result |
|---|---|
| `zoom < 0.35` and not Ctrl | **no snap** |
| (`zoom >= 0.8` or Ctrl) and `user_snap_enabled` | **full** `snap_to_grid` (round to `grid_size`, `strand_drawing_canvas.py:5168-5175`) |
| `0.5 <= zoom < 0.8` and `user_snap_enabled` | **gentle** snap only within `(grid_size/8)*zoom` of an intersection |
| Ctrl held with snap disabled in settings | **full snap** (Ctrl override) |
| else | no snap |

`user_snap_enabled` excludes bias controls (`4046-4048`). Early-out: if `snapped_pos == last_snapped_pos` (previous frame's snapped target), the whole update is skipped (`4086-4087`).

### 4.3 Endpoint update (`update_strand_position`, `move_mode.py:2702`, `2899-2975`)

```
if MaskedStrand(affected_strand): move_masked_strand(new_pos, side)        # (2901-2903) â€” dead by mouse
else:
    if side == 0: affected_strand.start = new_pos                          # (2928)
    else:         affected_strand.end   = new_pos                          # (2930)
                  if not control_point2_activated: cp2 = copy(new_pos)     # (2932-2937) passive cp2 follows
    update_shape(); update_side_line()
    for ms in truly_moving_strands where ms != affected_strand:            # (2950-2975)
        move_start/move_end per the move-test (Â§3.3); set ms.start/.end = new_pos
        if end-move and not ms.control_point2_activated: ms.cp2 = copy(new_pos)
```

### 4.4 Control points & auto-adjust

- **cp1** (`2796-2816`): `control_point1=new_pos`; reveal cp2 (`control_point2_shown=True`); do NOT recalc center.
- **cp2** (`2817-2843`): `control_point2=new_pos`; `control_point2_activated` toggles on whether cp2 sits within 1 px of end.
- **center** (`2844-2859`): `control_point_center=new_pos`; `control_point_center_locked=True`.
- **Coincident-CP carry (start/end setters, `strand.py:436-456`):** the start setter drags **BOTH** cp1 and cp2 if either `== old _start`; the end setter just stores. `AttachedStrand` mirrors this (`attached_strand.py:151-169`).
- **`auto_adjust_control_points` (`2469-2524`)**: runs **only at press** when `side=='control_point1'` AND cp1&cp2 are both within 1.0 px of start (collapsed triangle) â†’ snap cp2 to end, `control_point2_activated=False`, optionally lock center at midpoint (third-CP on). Its recursion into children is **dead code** (gated `if not is_moving_control_point`, but only reachable for `control_point1` which forces `is_moving_control_point=True`).
- **`update_shape` (`strand.py:743-796`)**: recomputes `control_point_center` as the cp midpoint **unless** `control_point_center_locked`; a locked center within 0.5 px of the default **auto-unlocks** (`767-780`).

### 4.5 Attached-child follow

The **live** per-move path drives motion via the LSM-gathered `truly_moving_strands` and only adds the immediate parent to the redraw set (`2944-2946`). The legacy helpers `update_all_attached_strands` (`3446-3480`, child.start follows parent oldâ†’new) and `update_parent_strands` (`3400-3444`, push child up via `attachment_side`) are **not** on the live per-mousemove path.

### 4.6 JS drag (`moveHandle`, `actions.ts:23-93`; `snapPoint`, `:15-19`)

`pos = snapPoint(cursor + drag.offset, settings)`; control points move alone with `recenter` (`42-61`); endpoint path early-returns if unchanged (`70`), `moveEnd` writes the endpoint and carries the **same-side** control point by `(dx,dy)` only if it was within `EPS=1e-3` of the old endpoint (`73-81`), then `connectedMovers` peers snap to the identical `pos` (`88-92`). `snapPoint` is an **unconditional** grid round with no zoom/Ctrl gating. These differences are the Â§7 drag-math gaps (snap gating, cp2-follow criterion, start-move dual-CP carry, center auto-unlock).

---

## 5. Masked-Layer Movement (the focus)

### 5.1 A MaskedStrand owns NO independent geometry

Its visible region is the live boolean intersection of two **stroked** constituent outlines, minus deletion rectangles: `custom_mask_path = stroked(first) âˆ© stroked(second) âˆ’ Î£ deletion-rect bbox` (`update_mask_path`, `masked_strand.py:926-1014`; outlines via `get_stroked_path_for_strand`, `366-382`). Persistent state that actually matters:

- `first_selected_strand` / `second_selected_strand` â€” **the source of truth** (their start/end drive the intersection).
- `deletion_rectangles` â€” list of 4-corner **absolute-coordinate** dicts.
- `base_center_point` / `edited_center_point` â€” path-sampled centroids via a **50Ã—50 grid** `path.contains` average (`_calculate_center_from_path`, `1143-1170`) â€” NOT a true area centroid.

The mask's own `start`/`end` (copied from `first_selected_strand` at construction, `36-37`) and `control_point1/2 = None` (`46-47`) are **vestigial bookkeeping** â€” never drawn, never drive rendering. `update_shape` is a no-op (`84-86`).

### 5.2 Two movement situations â€” they behave differently

**(1) CONSTITUENT-MOVE (the live runtime path).** Dragging a normal/attached strand `S` that is a mask's constituent runs the normal endpoint branch; then the fix-up block (`move_mode.py:2978-3031`):

```
all_moved = [affected_strand] + truly_moving_strands
for mask in canvas.strands where mask is MaskedStrand:
    if mask.first_selected_strand or second_selected_strand in all_moved:
        old_center = mask.edited_center_point or base_center_point      # snapshot BEFORE
        mask.update_mask_path(); mask.calculate_center_point()          # REBUILD intersection + centroids
        new_center = mask.edited_center_point or base_center_point
        dx,dy = new_center - old_center
        for r in mask.deletion_rectangles: r.{tl,tr,bl,br} += (dx,dy)   # translate by CENTER delta
        add mask to affected_strands; break
```

The mask **follows automatically** and is **recomputed (not stale)** every frame; the erased windows ride along with the **centroid** shift. Not guarded by `using_absolute_coords` â€” runs for runtime and JSON-loaded masks.

**(2) `move_masked_strand` (legacy / unreachable-by-mouse here).** If a mask were the `affected_strand` (it cannot be â€” Â§2.7), `move_masked_strand` (`3126-3256`) would set the mask's own start/end, set the **same side of BOTH constituents** to `new_pos` (preserving each opposite end), translate deletion rects by the mask-**ENDPOINT** delta, then `force_complete_update`. Because masks are filtered out of every hit-test, this is dead for mouse drag.

> **Delta-basis divergence to replicate:** the live path uses the mask-**CENTER (centroid)** delta; `move_masked_strand` uses the mask-**ENDPOINT** delta. A faithful port of runtime behavior must use the **centroid** delta.

### 5.3 Edge cases

- Non-overlapping constituents â†’ empty `custom_mask_path`, but `deletion_rectangles` are **preserved** (`947-951`); centroid falls back to base/endpoint midpoints (`1100-1139`).
- Centroid is a **50Ã—50 grid** sample â€” porting must replicate the sampling, not a true centroid, for pixel-exact rectangle drift.
- `deletion_rectangles` corners are absolute tuples (`top_left/top_right/bottom_left/bottom_right`); translation mutates all four in place.

### 5.4 What JS does (and where it diverges)

`moveHandle` **never mutates any MaskedStrand** (`actions.ts:23-93`): a mask can't be the grabbed strand (`hitTest` excludes `type==='MaskedStrand'`, `hitTest.ts:25-27`) and is never a peer (`buildConnTable` skips masks). The renderer **re-derives** every mask region per frame from the **live** OVER/UNDER component records (`drawMasked`/`maskRegion`/`maskComponentPath`, `strand-renderer.js:1047-1099, 835-855, 795-812`) â€” so the **intersection follows the dragged component automatically with zero mask mutation**. âœ… matches OSS.

**The divergence (Â§7):** JS stores `deletion_rectangles` as **absolute world coords** and `subtractDeletions` (`814-826`) applies them at **fixed** world positions. There is **no centroid recompute and no corner translation** on a single-strand move (only `translateGroup`/`rotateGroup` shift corners, `actions.ts:466-473, 637-647`). JS has no `edited_center_point`/`base_center_point`/`calculate_center_point` at all. **Net: dragging a masked component slides the ribbon UNDER a world-pinned erase window**, where OSS carries the window along by the centroid delta. The mask's stored `start/end` (copied at `createMask`, `actions.ts:935-939`) also go stale but are harmless (renderer ignores them).

---

## 6. Release & Rendering-While-Held

### 6.1 Timers (OSS)

Three 16 ms (~60 fps) `QTimer`s (`setup_timer`, `move_mode.py:128-145`): `move_timerâ†’gradual_move`, `hold_timerâ†’force_redraw_while_holding`, `redraw_timerâ†’force_continuous_redraw`. `hold_timer`/`redraw_timer` start on **button-down** (`1146,1151`), so continuous redraw is tied to **holding**, not motion. Each short-circuits once the snapped position stops changing (`force_redraw_while_holding` `1457-1459`; `force_continuous_redraw` `3976-3978`) â€” a settled stationary hold does almost no work. `force_continuous_redraw` always sets `background_cache_valid=False` (refreshes grid+all strands); `force_redraw_while_holding` resets `_frames_since_click=0`. (JS has **no equivalent timers** â€” its canvas fully repaints per pointermove and the doc doesn't mutate while held, so there is no observable difference; this was verified as a *non-gap*.)

### 6.2 Moving-set / background split (OSS optimized paint)

`_setup_optimized_paint_handler` (`399`) monkey-patches `canvas.paintEvent`. Layer 1 = transparent `background_cache` pixmap drawn **once** (background+grid + `static_strands`); with `draw_only_affected_strand=True` the static set is **empty** (`671-674`), MaskedStrand constituents are pulled OUT of static so they redraw live (`677-684`). Layer 2 = per-frame blit of the cache (`771-774`) + live `truly_moving_strands` redrawn on top. Masks force a cache rebuild **every frame** (`515-517`) and seed `truly_moving_strands` with both constituents (`614-621, 640-647`).

### 6.3 Quality toggle

The optimized handler paints `QPainter(self_canvas)` **directly to the widget** (`755`), **bypassing the 2Ã— supersampling buffer** (`strand_drawing_canvas.py:68-69, 1867-1892, 2831-2832`). Antialiasing stays on (`enable_high_quality=True`), but there is **no 2Ã— oversample during an active drag** â€” lower spatial quality, higher speed. Full-quality supersampled rendering resumes on release.

### 6.4 Release (`mouseReleaseEvent`, `move_mode.py:1595-1682`)

`reset_movement_state` (`1510-1593`) stops all three timers, zeroes movement state + `_is_being_moved` flags, restores `canvas.paintEvent`, and deletes temp attrs (`background_cache`, `truly_moving_strands`, `active_strand_for_drawing`, `_frames_since_click`). Selection is restored **atomically** (clear all `is_selected`, set the one final strand â€” masks via `selected_strand`, `1653-1671`). Two repaints forced (immediate + `singleShot(0)`) through the restored full-quality path. `triangle_has_moved` is reset to `False` if cp2 AND the center are within 1.0 px of start (`1620-1637`).

### 6.5 Commit (single undo step)

External: `undo_redo_manager.enhanced_mouse_release` (`3040-3060`) captures `was_moving` **before** the original release (which resets it), runs the original, then calls `save_state()` **exactly once and only if `was_moving`** (forcing `_last_save_time=0` for cp/bias moves). A non-moving click saves nothing. `cancel_movement` (`1491-1508`) is an external abort that resets + restores selection but **does NOT** `save_state` â€” no undo entry.

### 6.6 JS release/commit (`MoveMode.onPointerUp`, `MoveMode.ts:76-86`)

`drag=null; setDragging(false); setDragMoving([]); endWeldGesture(); commit(); requestRender()`. `commit` (`editorStore.ts:350-357`) pushes the gesture baseline iff the doc changed visibly â€” **one drag = one undo step**. `beginGesture` (`editorStore.ts:346`) snapshots the baseline (first-call-wins). The `triangle_has_moved` reset and a no-commit abort path are the Â§7 lifecycle gaps; JS wires `pointercancel` to `onPointerUp` (so cancel commits) and ESC does not abort the drag.

---

## 7. JS Fidelity Gap Table â€” CONFIRMED gaps only (most severe first)

| Dimension | Sev | OSS behavior | JS behavior | OSS ref | JS ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Grab: global CP-first pass** | major | Dedicated FIRST pass over ALL strands forward; first control-point hit anywhere returns before any endpoint is tested â€” a 50px CP rect on any layer beats a 120px endpoint area on any other layer. | `hitTest` returns the first handle of the topmost strand (CPs interleaved per-strand with that strand's endpoints); a top strand's endpoint wins over a lower strand's CP that also contains the click. | `move_mode.py:1223-1241`, `1814-1822` | `hitTest.ts:55-62` |
| **Grab: iteration direction** | major | CP pass and connection-aware endpoint pass iterate **FORWARD** (bottom-first); only the no-endpoint fallback iterates reverse. Overlapping CPs/endpoints resolve to the **bottom/forward** strand. | Single reversed (topmost-first) order for **both** handle and body passes; overlapping handles resolve to the **top** strand. | `move_mode.py:1223`, `1829-1887`, `1890` | `hitTest.ts:51-71` |
| **Grab: parent preference at a joint** | major | Connection-aware pass collects all joint peers and **prefers the first non-`AttachedStrand` (parent)**; a parent+child weld grabs the **parent** regardless of nearness/topmost-ness â€” this sets the directed BFS root. | No joint awareness; first topmost endpoint circle wins (typically the child, attached later â†’ on top). Different primary â‡’ different directed move set. | `move_mode.py:1829-1887` (`1873-1884`) | `MoveMode.ts:31-45`, `hitTest.ts:55-62` |
| **Body click selects** | major | Move-mode press has **no body hit-test**; a body click outside all endpoint/CP areas does **not** grab and **does not select** (blank branch re-asserts prior selection). | `hitTest` pass 2 body-hits and `setSelection({layerName, handle:null})` **selects** the clicked strand. | `move_mode.py:1814-1900` (no body test), `1428-1443` | `hitTest.ts:64-71` + `MoveMode.ts:47-49` |
| **Empty-space click selection** | major | Blank click **keeps** the prior selection (`is_selected = (strand == selection_at_press_start)`; canvas refs restored). | `setSelection({layerName:null,...})` **clears** `selected_strand_name` â€” blank click **deselects**. | `move_mode.py:1428-1443` | `MoveMode.ts:50-52` + `editorStore.ts:421-425` |
| **Mask deletion-rects don't follow component drag** | major | On a constituent endpoint drag, OSS recomputes `update_mask_path`+`calculate_center_point` and **translates every deletion rectangle by the centroid delta** so the erase window rides the moving ribbon. | `moveHandle` never touches any mask; deletion rects are absolute world coords subtracted at fixed positions; ribbon slides **under a stationary erase window**. | `move_mode.py:2978-3031` (`3015-3026`); `masked_strand.py:1090-1098, 1143-1170` | `actions.ts:23-93, 160-165`; `strand-renderer.js:814-826, 776-789` |
| **Snap zoom-bands + Ctrl override missing** | major | 5-way zoom/Ctrl gating: no snap at `zoom<0.35`; gentle band `0.5â€“0.8`; **Ctrl forces full snap** even when the setting is OFF; bias controls excluded. | Unconditional `round(p/g)*g` whenever `snap_to_grid_enabled`; no zoom dependence, no Ctrl override (`p.ctrl` ignored), no bias carve-out. *(Zoom bands latent until zoom is unpinned from 1.0; the Ctrl-override gap is live today.)* | `move_mode.py:4036-4079` | `actions.ts:15-19` + `MoveMode.ts:59-60` |
| **Endpoint grab area shape** | minor | 120Ã—120 **square** (`contains`); corners reach ~84.85px; area 14400 pxÂ². Overlay even draws the square. | 60px-**radius circle** (`hypot<=60`); corner clicks (e.g. (59,59)â‰ˆ83px) rejected; area ~11310 pxÂ² (~21% smaller). | `move_mode.py:2155-2206` | `hitTest.ts:17,21,59-60` |
| **CP grab area shape** | minor | 50Ã—50 **square** (`contains`); corners to ~35.36px; area 2500 pxÂ². | 25px-**radius circle**; corner clicks rejected; area ~1963 pxÂ². | `move_mode.py:2136-2153` | `hitTest.ts:18,59` |
| **cp1 collapsed-triangle auto-adjust at press** | minor | At press, grabbing cp1 on a collapsed triangle snaps **cp2â†’end** + `control_point2_activated=False` (+ optional center lock) â€” a press-time geometry mutation. | No press-time mutation; cp1's move-path sets `triangle_has_moved`/`control_point2_shown` but never snaps cp2â†’end or clears `activated`. | `move_mode.py:2469-2524` (`2517-2518`) | `MoveMode.ts:32-46` + `actions.ts:42-48` |
| **cp2 grabbable via separation fallback** | minor | cp2 grabbable **only if `control_point2_shown`**; else falls through to endpoints. | `control_point2_shown || sep(cp2,start,end)` (>6px from both endpoints) â€” a loaded strand with cp2 far from ends but `control_point2_shown=false` makes cp2 grabbable in JS, not OSS. | `move_mode.py:2058-2071` | `hitTest.ts:22-23,45` |
| **center-handle grab gate** | minor | Grabbable only when **`enable_third_control_point` AND `triangle_has_moved`**; lock is a *consequence* of grabbing, not a precondition. | Grabbable only when `control_point_center_locked`, which is set **only by dragging the center** â€” chicken-and-egg â‡’ effectively never grabbable via fresh interaction (only from loaded `locked:true`). | `move_mode.py:2080-2096, 2144-2150` | `hitTest.ts:32-34` |
| **START move carries only cp1, not cp2** | minor | start setter carries **BOTH** cp1 and cp2 if either `== old_start` (default fresh-strand state in OSS: cp1==cp2==start). | `moveEnd('start')` carries only `control_points[0]`; a cp2 on the old start is left behind. *(Latent because JS factory seeds cp2=end, itself a separate init divergence.)* | `strand.py:436-444`; `attached_strand.py:151-160`; `move_mode.py:2962-2963` | `actions.ts:73-81` |
| **END-move cp2-follow criterion** | minor | cp2 snaps to the new endpoint iff **`control_point2_activated==False`** (the persisted FLAG), regardless of cp2's position. | cp2 carried by delta iff within `EPS=1e-3` of the **old** endpoint (POSITION); the flag is never read. Diverges for passive-but-displaced cp2 and activated-but-coincident cp2. | `strand.py:452-456`; `move_mode.py:2932-2937, 2967-2972` | `actions.ts:73-81` |
| **cp2 not deactivated on drag-back-to-endpoint** | minor | Dragging cp2 within 1.0px of the endpoint sets `control_point2_activated=False` (re-enters passive). | cp2 drag **unconditionally** sets `control_point2_activated=true`; combined with the position-based end-move gate, a later end-move won't auto-carry cp2 in the ~1px..1e-3px band (snap-off case). | `move_mode.py:2821-2831` | `actions.ts:49-53` |
| **center lock never auto-unlocks** | minor | `update_shape` auto-unlocks `control_point_center_locked` when the center returns within 0.5px of the cp midpoint (also fires when cp1/cp2 move so the midpoint reaches the locked center). | `recenter` only re-derives when unlocked; the lock is never cleared at runtime â‡’ a center dragged back onto the midpoint stays pinned. | `strand.py:767-780` | `actions.ts:33-40` |
| **`triangle_has_moved` not reset on release** | minor | Reset to `False` on release when the curve returns to straight (cp2 & center within 1.0px of start), re-hiding the gated handle. | Never reset on release; the actually-stuck JS flag is **`control_point2_shown`** (also never reset), so cp2 stays grabbable/visible after un-shaping. | `move_mode.py:1620-1637` | `MoveMode.ts:76-86` |
| **No abort-without-commit path** | minor | `cancel_movement` resets + restores selection **without** `save_state` â€” an aborted move leaves no undo entry. | `pointercancel` is wired to `onPointerUp`, which always `commit()`s (an undo step if visibly changed); ESC clears selection but does not abort the `drag`. No no-commit abort. | `move_mode.py:1491-1508` | `InteractionHost.ts:33,182-188` + `MoveMode.ts:76-86` |
| **No-op early-out semantics** | cosmetic | Early-out compares the snapped target to the **previous frame's** snapped pos, for all sides. | Early-out only on endpoints vs the **current endpoint** coord; CP branches have no early-out; no cross-frame memory. (Same resulting geometry â€” perf/short-circuit only.) | `move_mode.py:4086-4087` | `actions.ts:69-70` |

> Several minor gaps describe the **same root code paths** from different dimensions (e.g. start-move cp1-only carry, end-move flag-vs-position, mask deletion-rect translation appear under both `connections`/`dragmath`/`masks`/`lifecycle`). They are listed once each above; fixing the underlying functions resolves all aliases.

### Recommended fixes

1. **Grab priority + geometry (one rewrite of `hitTest`/`MoveMode.onPointerDown`)** â€” biggest fidelity win:
   - Add a **global control-point pass over ALL strands FORWARD** that returns before any endpoint test; then a **forward connection-aware endpoint pass** that collects joint peers and **prefers the first non-`AttachedStrand` (parent)**; then a **reverse fallback** for isolated endpoints (only if no endpoint area matched). This fixes the global-CP, iteration-direction, and parent-preference gaps together and corrects the directed BFS root.
   - Replace circular `near()` grab tests with **axis-aligned square containment** (120Ã—120 endpoints, 50Ã—50 CPs) to match `QPainterPath/QRectF.contains`.
2. **Selection semantics in `MoveMode.onPointerDown`**: **remove the body hit-test branch** for move mode (a body click must not grab/select); on a blank click **re-assert the prior selection** instead of clearing it.
3. **Mask deletion-rectangle tracking**: in `moveHandle` (or a post-move hook), after moving constituents, find each MaskedStrand whose component moved, recompute its **50Ã—50-grid centroid** (port `_calculate_center_from_path` + `update_mask_path`), and **translate `deletion_rectangles` by the centroid delta**. Add `base_center_point`/`edited_center_point` to the mask record so the delta is well-defined frame-to-frame.
4. **Snapping**: gate `snapPoint` on **zoom bands** and add the **Ctrl-forced full-snap override** (read `p.ctrl`, thread `view.zoom` into `Settings`/snap), excluding bias controls. The Ctrl override is live today; the zoom bands future-proof for unpinned zoom.
5. **Control-point flag fidelity** (cluster): on END moves, gate cp2-follow on **`control_point2_activated`** (not position); on cp2 drag-back within 1.0px, set `control_point2_activated=false`; on START moves, carry **both** cp1 and cp2 if either coincided with the old start; add center **auto-unlock** within 0.5px of the cp midpoint; reset **`control_point2_shown`** (and re-derive center) on release when the curve returns to straight; port the press-time **cp1 collapsed-triangle auto-adjust**.
6. **Abort path**: route `pointercancel` (and an ESC-during-drag handler) to a `cancelMove()` that resets `drag`/`setDragging`/`endWeldGesture` and **does not** `commit()` â€” mirroring `cancel_movement`'s no-undo abort.
